titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward Marsh to the Nicolas Lamb, 8 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: E. Marsh

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
A letter from Edward Marsh to the Nicolas Lamb, 8 July 1916. Marsh writes on behalf of Prime Minister H. H. Asquith to acknowledge the receipt of a letter from Lamb.
Edward Marsh (1872–1953) was the Assistant Private Secretary to Prime Minister H. H. Asquith. He later had a long association with Winston Churchill and was knighted in 1937.
Nicolas Lamb (b. 1861) was the Town Clerk of Longford and sat on the Longford Urban District Council. The Longford Urban District Council was the towns local authority and was renamed the Longford Town Council in 2002.

correspAction.placeName: 10 Downing Street, Whitehall, S.W., London, England
Longford Urban District Council, Longford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
10 Downing St.
Whitehall S.W 8 July 1916 Dear Sir The Prime Minister desires me
to acknowledge receipt of your letter of the
5th inst and of the enclosed resolution E. Marsh Nicholas Lamb
